title: COVERAGE UNDER PMJUGA IN KARNATAKA
text:
Press Release Page | Press Information Bureau

Ministry of Tribal Affairs

COVERAGE UNDER PMJUGA IN KARNATAKA

Posted On:
02 APR 2026 4:02PM by PIB Delhi

Union Minister of State for Tribal Affairs Shri Durgadas Uikey today informed Lok Sabha that
Pradhan Mantri Janjatiya Unnat Gram Abhiyan (PMJUGA), launched by the Hon’ble PM on October 2, 2024, as Dharti Aaba Janjatiya Gram Utkarsh Abhiyan, aims
 to saturate infrastructural gaps, improve access to health, education, Anganwadi facilities and providing livelihood opportunities to
 tribal people of 63,843 villages
 in 549 districts and 2,911 blocks
 across 30 States/UTs including one village in Kodagu district and 62 villages in Mysuru district of the State of Karnataka. The abhiyan include 25 interventions of 17 Ministries.

As per the information received from the line Ministries under DAJGUA, the details of various interventions undertaken in Mysuru and Kodagu districts of Karnataka under the DAJGUA scheme are placed at
Annexure-I
.

A total amount of ₹14,516.17 lakh has been sanctioned to the Government of Karnataka by the Ministry of Tribal Affairs under the DAJGUA scheme for various activities. Out of the total sanctioned amount, ₹2,181.00 lakh has been allocated for Mysuru district and ₹2,382.50 lakh for Kodagu district during the last two years. As on date, an amount of ₹2,877.50 lakh has been released to the Government of Karnataka by the Ministry of Tribal Affairs under DAJGUA.

As per the information received from the line Ministries under DAJGUA, achievement is placed at
Annexure 1.

Regular review meetings with Ministries/Departments and States/UTs under DAJGUA are conducted to assess the progress of the Abhiyan. These meetings also facilitate monitoring of both physical and financial progress of implementation at the Ministry as well as State/UT levels. Further, Project Monitoring Units (PMUs) have been established at the State and District levels to ensure effective monitoring.

Annexure I
